summary: Deprecated audit trail for the observed WPBT registry write. Microsoft documentation captured in this dataset publishes WPBT as a UEFI or DFCI managed feature, while the Session Manager registry path remains a community-sourced implementation detail.

setting.name: Disable WPBT Execution
setting.casual_explanation: WPBT lets vendors and OEMs run a program from firmware during Windows startup. This tweak currently relies on a Session Manager registry value that is widely cited in community hardening material, but this research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft document that publishes that exact registry mapping.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want to reduce OEM or firmware-driven startup execution paths and block unwanted vendor software from launching during boot.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The hardening intent is understandable, but the exact registry contract is not published in the Microsoft sources captured so far. That means this should stay a research-only record rather than a validated preset.
setting.professional_notes: The current app writes HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\DisableWpbtExecution = 1. Repo notes and external community material tie that value to WPBT blocking behavior, but the Microsoft documentation captured in this dataset only publishes WPBT as a UEFI or DFCI managed setting with Not configured, Enabled, and Disabled states. Until a primary Microsoft source connects DisableWpbtExecution to WPBT, this record should track the registry write as an observed implementation only.

allowed_values.label: Default or unmanaged state
allowed_values.meaning: This research pass did not capture a primary Microsoft document that defines the default behavior for this registry value.

allowed_values.label: Observed app profile
allowed_values.meaning: The app writes DisableWpbtExecution = 1 and community hardening references describe that as blocking WPBT execution, but this registry mapping is not yet backed by a primary Microsoft source in this dataset.

targets.notes: Official Microsoft documentation captured so far publishes WPBT as a UEFI or DFCI feature surface, not as this registry value.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes DisableWpbtExecution = 1 under HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager. This record intentionally documents that exact registry write as an observed implementation only.

windows_defaults.label: Conservative baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Systems where WPBT management should only be published from an official Microsoft surface

states.rationale: Do not publish this registry path as the supported WPBT control until a primary Microsoft mapping is captured.

windows_defaults.notes: This record keeps the observed registry write separate from the official UEFI or DFCI feature surface.

recommended_profiles.label: Hold for research

states.rationale: Keep the public record conservative until the registry mapping is backed by a primary Microsoft source.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to preserve the observed implementation without overstating confidence.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a validated and officially documented WPBT control today.
tradeoffs: The observed registry write remains documented.
The record does not publish an unsupported registry path as a validated control.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn DFCI WPBT setting reference
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents WPBT as a UEFI or DFCI managed feature with Not configured, Enabled, and Disabled states.

evidence.title: Repo security notes for WPBT
evidence.summary: The repo security notes track DisableWpbtExecution under Session Manager as the current implementation surface and cite community hardening references for the mapping.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Session Manager\DisableWpbtExecution = 1 for the WPBT tweak.

decision.why: The observed registry write is clear, but the Microsoft documentation captured in this dataset does not yet publish DisableWpbtExecution as the supported WPBT control surface.
blocking_issues: No primary Microsoft source in this dataset maps DisableWpbtExecution under Session Manager as the supported WPBT control surface.
The official Microsoft surface captured so far is UEFI or DFCI oriented rather than a desktop registry policy.

open_questions: Does Microsoft publish DisableWpbtExecution in a Windows security, boot, or firmware reference outside the DFCI material captured so far?
If not, should this tweak remain docs-first permanently or be retired in favor of officially managed firmware controls?

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: Microsoft Learn DFCI WPBT setting reference: Microsoft documents WPBT as a UEFI or DFCI managed feature with Not configured, Enabled, and Disabled states.
validation_proof.notes: Backfilled from evidence_id ms-dfci-wpbt (official-doc); deprecated audit trail.
